evidence_quotes.quote: For many Video-games are the place of escape from harsh reality and the hardness of everyday life.

paragraphs.text: This proposed initiative is of at upmost importance. Not only for the interests and protection of European citizens but also for the whole Eurozone. As it not only aims to prevent the destruction of culture but also preventing corporate cartels. Now Imagine going to the store after a long day of work. After you come back home to your loving and hungry family. You put the shopping bag on the kitchen table, and as you open it, in intention to start cooking a meal, you realize that already about half of the items you bought just a moment ago. Are all gone. Vanished. Without a Trace. How would you feel? Angry? Confused? Betrayed, even? You start checking everywhere but they're gone, for good. And so is your hard earned money. You paid for nothing, literally. That is how over a million European citizens who signed this initiative feels. Our beloved pastime activity. ROBBED FROM US. For many Video-games are the place of escape from harsh reality and the hardness of everyday life. Not only are they places where you can steam off and have fun. They're places that unites the people, where us humans make friends and meet them again and again time to time. When distance separates us, games unite us. All in same place. No man, Woman nor a child should be the victim of theft of any kind. Permanently deleting a game that could be held up by individuals without any extra costs to the publishers nor the developers should be counted as a theft or cultural destruction. Games aren't just a place where anyone and everyone can come and enjoy, they're visual novels. And we love them. The question is, does the EU Commission love us?
